# DESIGN_PRINCIPLES.md — design-first-demo
*Generated by Layer 0 prompt. Reviewed and corrected manually.*

## Coding Conventions
- Controllers delegate immediately — no logic beyond validation and service call
- Services own all business logic — no direct repository or framework calls from controllers
- All public methods have Javadoc — one sentence minimum
- Constructor injection only — no `@Autowired` on fields
- Records for DTOs — no mutable POJOs for request/response objects
- Explicit types — no `var` keyword

## Naming Patterns
| Element | Pattern | Example |
|---------|---------|---------|
| Controllers | `{Resource}Controller` | `QuestionController` |
| Services | `{Resource}Service` | `QuestionService` |
| DTOs | `{Action}{Resource}Request/Response` | `AskRequest`, `AskResponse` |
| Exceptions | descriptive + `Exception` | `VectorStoreException`, `ModelException` |
| Config records | `{Feature}Properties` | `RagProperties` |
| Tests | `{Class}Test` | `QuestionServiceTest` |
| Test methods | `method_condition_expectedResult` | `findAnswer_noDocumentsFound_returnsDefaultAnswer` |

## State Management
No application state. All state lives in the vector store (managed externally). Service methods are stateless — no instance variables except injected dependencies.

## Error Handling
- Named exception per failure category (not `RuntimeException` with a message string)
- Exceptions thrown at the boundary where the external call lives
- `GlobalExceptionHandler` maps exceptions to HTTP — mapping never lives in service code
- Log once at the boundary (`log.error(msg, ex)`) — never log and rethrow
- Swallow nothing — empty catch blocks are a build failure

## Testing Approach
- Service layer: `@ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class)`, no Spring context, Mockito for all dependencies
- Controller layer: `@WebMvcTest`, `@MockBean` for service, `MockMvc` for HTTP assertions
- What is tested: happy path, empty result, each exception type, input validation
- What is not tested: Spring wiring, configuration loading, PGVector behavior

## Design Constraints
- Do not use `@Autowired` on fields — constructor injection only
- Do not put business logic in controllers — delegate to service immediately
- Do not use `var` — explicit types required
- Do not use `@SpringBootTest` for unit tests — use `@WebMvcTest` for controller layer
- Do not assert on exact error message strings in tests — assert on error codes or HTTP status
- Do not create service interfaces — `@Service` classes directly, no interface unless multiple implementations exist
